sources_exist.note: All 11 URL occurrences resolved to accessible pages.

figures_match_sources.note: Zenfolio supports the AI-use, age, and product-mix figures; Capturely supports the CADY acquisition, PE ownership, school RFP/commission structure, and Apollo ownership; Apollo supports the $2.7B enterprise value. BizBuySell does not report photography-studio SDE multiples, Apollo does not report EBITDA needed for 6.5x, Zenfolio does not support the remembered 1999-2005 digital-transition curve, and Capturely does not establish two active consolidators.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Within-role fractions, pi_dist=0.35, and pi_moat=0.25 are bounded and directionally conservative. However, t50=1.5 partly rests on an unsourced film-to-digital timeline, and all three ESTIMATE inputs—buy_mult, exit_mult, and historical_analogs—lack the evidence needed to validate their transformations. This explicitly resolves the ESTIMATE_HEAVY flag as adverse.

rubric_consistent.note: Dataset inputs match and formula structure is correct. S=3.091339 is 0.0913 above the 3.00 pass cut, resolving the assigned BORDERLINE flag. C=0.875 is below the frozen any-factor-below-1.0 kill threshold, and the MELTING class caps the result at pass; the record discusses the binding C and melting thesis honestly. But unsupported B/M/A research inputs prevent rubric-consistent acceptance.

cross_checks_honest.note: All mandatory cross-checks are present and candid: gross uplift is separated from capture, MELTING is segmented with the 60-70% occasion-locked share explicitly labeled an estimate, PE evidence is confined to B/M, and competitive/RFP deflation is assigned to C. Stored-versus-recomputed deltas are only V=4.0e-8, B=-1.50e-7, and S=3.21e-7, so the ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is benign.

source_audits.note: Capturely states that CADY, backed by Trivest, acquired Lifetouch high-school accounts and Prestige in August 2025, and identifies Lifetouch/Shutterfly as Apollo-owned. It does not show that Apollo/Shutterfly completed an add-on in the last 24 months, so it establishes one qualifying active acquirer, not value=2.

BizBuySell's accessible industry table has no photography-studio category and does not state the quoted 1.5x-3.0x photography SDE range or the cited example listing. It therefore cannot support the conversion to buy_mult=3.5x normalized EBITDA.

Zenfolio reports AI non-use falling from 46% to 18% and function use of 44.6% for noise reduction, 42.8% for background removal, and 39.2% for auto-subject selection, supporting the declared 42% material-editing proxy.

Apollo's release supports Shutterfly's approximately $2.7B enterprise value and $51 cash price but contains no adjusted EBITDA or guidance. It cannot establish the record's inferred 6.5x EV/EBITDA multiple.

Zenfolio supports the recent AI curve—12% more occasional/as-needed users and non-use falling from 46% to 18%—but does not support the claimed 1999-2005 film-to-digital majority-adoption timeline. The record explicitly admits no source for that memory-based analog.

The survey's age distribution reports 28.3% age 60 or older and 77% age 40 or older, directly supporting owners_60plus_pct=0.283 as a photographer/respondent proxy, with the stated owner-operator frame caveat.

The page describes annual school-vendor cycles, RFP evaluation, parent-purchase revenue, and school commissions of roughly 15%-40%+, supporting the procurement gatekeeper and commission pass-through mechanism.

Zenfolio identifies finding clients and determining prices as leading client-photographer concerns and shows broad self-employment, supporting a competitive retail market, though it does not quantify acquisition-related client attrition.

The survey documents widespread AI use including photo generation, editing, background removal, and culling, supporting rapid erosion of production-skill differentiation, although it does not itself measure consumer substitution by headshot apps.

Capturely documents the physical school logistics, contract/commission relationships, and new virtual at-home portrait model, supporting both an occasion/logistics remnant and technology-driven bypass pressure.

Zenfolio reports 52% offering mostly digital plus some print, 24% digital-only, and 4% physical-only, directly supporting the retail product mix. School per-child commissions are supported separately by Capturely, not by this URL.

reasons: Replace inputs.buy_mult.url with photography-studio transaction evidence that directly reports the SDE range and document the owner-labor-to-normalized-EBITDA conversion; https://www.bizbuysell.com/learning-center/industry-valuation-multiples/ contains no photography category or quoted example.

Add the missing EBITDA source for inputs.exit_mult or remove the inferred 6.5x claim; https://ir.apollo.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/188/funds-managed-by-affiliates-of-apollo-global-management supports $2.7B enterprise value but no EBITDA denominator.

Re-source inputs.historical_analogs with a publication that actually establishes the film-to-digital adoption timeline used in inputs.t50_years; https://zenfolio.com/sopi/2024-april/ supports only the 2023-2024 AI-use curve.

Correct inputs.active_consolidators.value or provide evidence for a second PE-backed platform completing add-ons in the last 24 months; https://capturely.com/best-school-photography-companies/ establishes CADY's recent acquisition but not recent add-on activity by Apollo/Shutterfly.
Re-run all score outputs after correcting the ESTIMATE-heavy buy_mult, exit_mult, and historical_analogs inputs; do not change the frozen formulas or thresholds.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 3 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (buy_mult, exit_mult, historical_analogs)
ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, S, V
